summary: First run: comprehensive backend + frontend testing of Customer Registry Pro. 19/19 pytest cases passed (auth, dashboard, customers CRUD + cascade delete, complaints partial updates/comments/attachments/timeline, tickets, interactions, notifications, search). Frontend login → dashboard flow works; dashboard KPIs, charts and pie render correctly with real seeded data (48 customers / 60 complaints / 12 open / 9 critical). Customers list/search/status-filter, complaints Kanban (5 cols / 60 cards) + table view toggle, complaint drawer with status/priority selects & Comments/Timeline/Files/Resolution tabs, Quick Add tabs, Global Search, Notifications mark-all-read, Reports (all 4 export buttons render, CSV download triggered successfully), Settings profile tab all verified via Playwright.

minor.issue: Empty q raises 422 (FastAPI required-query behavior); code inside handler already guards len<2. Recommend making q Optional so empty string returns empty payload as documented in the spec.

ui_bugs.issue: Clicking anywhere on the <tr> does NOT open the customer drawer — only clicking the name/avatar button inside the row opens it. The `data-testid=customer-row-{id}` therefore is not directly clickable to open the profile drawer as the review spec implies. Consider making the whole row clickable or add a dedicated 'view' button with a testid.

issues: Recharts logs 10x warnings 'width(-1)/height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on first paint before ResponsiveContainer measures its parent. Not a functional issue but noisy in console; consider giving containers a minHeight.

Displays admin user's name as 'Hema Priya' instead of the seeded 'Admin'. Likely because a prior PUT /auth/profile in a previous session modified the seeded admin's name. seed_admin() only re-syncs password, not name — so once mutated, the display name persists. Optional: also reset admin name on startup, or leave as-is.

action_items: (Optional) Make GET /api/search q parameter Optional with default '' so empty q returns {customers:[],complaints:[],tickets:[]} instead of 422.
(Optional low) Make the entire customer table row open the profile drawer (currently only the name button does).
(Optional low) In seed_admin, also reset name to 'Admin' if changed, or accept current behaviour (mutable profile).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 812 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting into routers/ (auth.py, customers.py, complaints.py, etc.) and models.py before it grows further.
MongoDB _id is consistently projected out via {'_id': 0} — good.
JWT expiry is 7 days; consider refresh-token flow for enterprise.
Password reset token is stored but there is no POST /api/auth/reset-password endpoint to consume it — the flow is incomplete (only forgot-password half exists).
add_attachment stores base64 in the same complaints document — for a 5MB file this bloats the doc; consider GridFS or S3 for real prod.
verify_password swallows all exceptions and returns False — masks real bugs; log the exception at debug level.
Cascade delete on customer removes complaints/tickets/interactions but not any orphaned attachment blobs (they live inside complaints so ok in this case).
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seed (48 customers, 60 complaints, 24 tickets, 80 interactions, 5 notifications). Test artefacts prefixed TEST_ were cleaned up in test lifecycle.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (pytest, 19 tests, parallel-safe with pytest-xdist). Admin login: admin@registry.pro / Admin@12345. Frontend uses localStorage.crp_token for auth, testids follow the naming in the review request. Kanban drag-drop was verified via PUT /api/complaints/{id} (status/priority partial update works) as suggested by the review note. change-password button testid exists on Settings but only after switching to the Security tab. Reports export triggered a real download for customers.csv.
rca of the issue: N/A — no critical bugs found. Minor items above are documented for optional follow-up.
